claims.claim_text: CEFR-aligned language assessment and reporting is a valid structure for workplace language testing.
claims.notes: CEFR-based corporate assessment products exist, and Allingua, Pearson and Pipplet explicitly position their tests around workplace assessment and CEFR reporting. The exact combined audit product is still an internal product design.
claims.next_action: re-search and verify with stricter source tier rules

claims.notes: No primary source reviewed here confirmed this exact figure.
claims.next_action: re-search and verify with stricter source tier rules

claims.claim_text: Pearson, Cambridge and Pipplet are generic, English-focused competitors.
claims.notes: Pearson and Pipplet both offer broader multilingual assessment routes and CEFR-aligned reporting; Allingua also offers adaptive testing in 29 languages with HR reporting. The market is not English-only.
claims.next_action: falsify fully and rewrite or drop

claims.claim_text: Nobody does a Luxembourg-specific trilingual language audit.
claims.notes: The absolute uniqueness claim is too strong because multilingual assessment and reporting products already exist in Luxembourg, even if no identical trilingual Luxembourg-specific package was found.
claims.next_action: falsify fully and rewrite or drop
